frameworks.name: predictive coding / perceptual expectation
frameworks.one_line: repetition suppression interpreted as fulfillment of top-down expectations about repeats

frameworks.name: bottom-up adaptation
frameworks.one_line: repetition suppression as local stimulus-driven neural fatigue without expectation modulation

frameworks.name: repetition probability paradigm
frameworks.one_line: human fMRI design adapted to macaque single-unit/LFP recordings with attended faces

core_question: Does the probability of a stimulus repetition modulate repetition suppression in macaque IT cortex when faces are attended?
key_assumptions: face patches in IT mirror human face-responsive cortex
attended faces engage top-down expectation signals if present
single-unit spikes and high gamma LFPs index local face processing
manipulations: repetition probability across blocks (high vs low)
task relevance of repetitions for behavioral decisions
use of faces as familiar attended stimuli
key_findings: significant repetition suppression in spiking activity and high gamma LFPs in both monkeys
no modulation of repetition suppression by repetition probability
no effect even when repetitions were task-relevant and influenced behavior
interpretations: macaque IT repetition suppression is not driven by perceptual expectation
human fMRI repetition-probability effects do not have a single-unit signature in macaque ML
limitations_noted: data from two monkeys, one face patch (ML) and one face-responsive region
